messages.content: Hey everyone! Random question—what’s the funniest thing you remember doing as a kid? I need a good laugh today 😂
Omg I have so many, but top of my list is trying to “bake” a mud pie and actually serving it to my neighbor like it was a fancy dessert. She legit pretended to eat it too, bless her 😂

Lol Iris, that’s amazing 😂 I remember once I tried to build a “parachute” out of plastic bags and jumped off the porch. I was convinced I’d just gently float down… nope, just a very ungraceful thud. My mom was NOT impressed.

Frank, that is so classic! I feel like every kid tries the plastic bag parachute at least once 😂 Did you at least stick the landing? I also remember trying to “invent” roller skates with blocks and string… let’s just say my shins paid the price for my creativity.

Omg these are gold 😂 My top one is probably when I tried to cut my own bangs with those tiny craft scissors… and ended up with this jagged little patch right in the middle of my forehead. My mom still has the school picture as blackmail evidence 😅

These are killing me 😂 I definitely tried the self-haircut thing too, Diana, except I thought it’d be a great idea to use a disposable razor… Let’s just say I had a lopsided “fade” for a month and my dad still brings it up at every family gathering. Kids are wild, honestly!

Omg, you guys are cracking me up 😂 I totally had a phase where I thought I was a magician and tried to make my little brother “disappear” by convincing him to crawl under a blanket and then just… leaving him there. He was SO mad when he realized I hadn’t actually done any magic at all. Honestly, surprised he still talks to me!

These are all too good 😂 I feel like every childhood had at least one disastrous “magic show” moment! Mine was when I tried to convince my sister she could fly if she jumped off the couch with an umbrella—Mary Poppins style. The only thing that flew was the umbrella when it snapped in half mid-air. She still brings it up whenever it rains 😅

Eve, that’s hilarious! The Mary Poppins umbrella move is a rite of passage 😂 I swear, the things we thought would work as kids… I also once tried to “train” my cat to pull me around in a cardboard box like a sled. She was NOT having it, but I was fully committed to turning her into a tiny reindeer. (Spoiler: it ended with scratches and me apologizing with treats for a week!)

These stories are making my day 😂 I remember trying to dig a “secret tunnel” in my backyard with a plastic shovel because I was convinced I’d end up in China. Got about a foot down before I hit a root and gave up—so my “tunnel” just became a random hole my dad kept tripping over. Not quite the international journey I had in mind!

Haha these are so good 😂 I think I was maybe the least adventurous, because my idea of wild was making everyone play board games for HOURS. I’d get super competitive over Candy Land and Monopoly—my family still refuses to play with me because apparently I “take it too seriously” lol. No parachute jumps or magic shows, just intense dice rolling and dramatic arguments over who gets Boardwalk 😅

Bob, honestly, you were just preparing for future business negotiations with that Monopoly hustle 😂 I was the kid who tried to invent new board game rules halfway through and then argued they were “official.” Pretty sure my siblings still have trust issues because of my so-called "house rules" in Uno.

Henry, I totally relate to the “house rules” chaos 😂 My cousin and I used to make up new rules for every game just so we could win faster, and then act all shocked when the adults called us out. Honestly, I think half the fun was just making everyone else super confused. Childhood games really brought out everyone’s inner lawyer!

Totally agree, Grace! I feel like every game night turned into a full-on courtroom drama with everyone presenting their case 😂 Also, does anyone else remember making “potions” in the backyard with mud, leaves, and whatever random stuff you could find? I was absolutely convinced I was a witch brewing something magical—my poor mom never knew what she’d find in her good mixing bowls!

Eve, YES! The backyard “potion” phase was real 😂 I’d mix mud, grass, and those little helicopter seeds, then try to convince my sister it would turn her into a superhero if she touched it. Spoiler: it just made a big mess and got me banned from using the garden hose for a week. I seriously thought I was a scientist though—mad experiments everywhere!

Omg, the “potion” stage was a whole chapter of my childhood too 😂 I used to collect dandelions and mash them up with water thinking I’d invented some kind of magical perfume. Spoiler: it just smelled like lawn clippings and my hands were stained yellow for days. My mom still mentions the time I secretly mixed up a batch in her fancy teacups… I am so sorry, Mom!

Haha Iris, the fancy teacup incident is classic! I definitely ruined a few nice kitchen things in the name of “science” too. I once tried to microwave crayons to make new colors and just ended up with a rainbow mess everywhere—and a very unimpressed parent. I was banned from kitchen experiments after that one 😅

Grace, microwaving crayons is bold 😂 I can just imagine the look on your parent’s face when they opened the microwave! Did anyone else ever try to “cook” with random stuff in the kitchen? I remember making a “cake” out of bread, orange juice, and jelly because I thought I was a culinary genius… My parents were good sports and pretended to taste it, but the faces they made told the real story.

Bob, I definitely tried to cook up some questionable masterpieces as a kid! I remember mixing flour, ketchup, and sprinkles because I thought “sprinkles make everything better” 😂 My little brother and I served it to our stuffed animals, but I’m pretty sure even they would’ve passed if they could! Kids’ cooking shows would be absolute chaos if they let us run wild like that!

Omg, yes! I totally had a phase where I’d “invent recipes” and force my family to have a “restaurant night”—the menu was always like, toast with weird toppings or “fancy” water with lemon slices (aka whatever fruit I could sneak). I was very proud of my soggy cracker creations 😂 Looking back, I have no idea how my parents kept a straight face through all those “gourmet” meals!
Haha Alice, soggy cracker appetizers, love it 😂 I definitely remember putting way too much

I’d get super competitive over Candy Land and Monopoly—my family still refuses to play with me because apparently I “take it too seriously” lol. No parachute jumps or magic shows, just intense dice rolling and dramatic arguments over who gets Boardwalk 😅
